samples.target_compile_errors:
error[E0277]: `main` has invalid return type `i32`
 --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/expressions/rel_op_c6b/sample_001/target.rs:6:14
  |
6 | fn main() -> i32 {
  |              ^^^ `main` can only return types that implement `Termination`
  |
  = help: consider using `()`, or a `Result`

error[E0133]: call to unsafe function `std::ptr::const_ptr::<impl *const T>::add` is unsafe and requires unsafe function or block
  --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/expressions/rel_op_c6b/sample_001/target.rs:22:16
   |
22 |     let end2 = a.as_ptr().add(3);
   |                ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ call to unsafe function
   |
   = note: consult the function's documentation for information on how to avoid undefined behavior

error: this operation will panic at runtime
  --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/expressions/rel_op_c6b/sample_001/target.rs:21:17
   |
21 |     let end1 = &a[3] as *const i32;
   |                 ^^^^ index out of bounds: the length is 3 but the index is 3
   |
   = note: `#[deny(unconditional_panic)]` on by default

error: aborting due to 3 previous errors

Some errors have detailed explanations: E0133, E0277.
For more information about an error, try `rustc --explain E0133`.
